preamble: The transformation of voiced dental stop [d] to palatal nasal [ɲ] involves concurrent nasalization and palatalization, typically under the influence of neighboring nasal or front vowel segments.

examples.note: Historical Slavic developments often showed dental stops nasalizing and palatalizing into [ɲ] in specific clusters.
Certain Romance dialects exhibit nasalization and palatalization of dental stops.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
